name: design-rules
title: Design rules (law + craft)
description: The two files that govern designing with Pucar: design-guidelines.md (the law â€” teal rationing, sentence case, AA, colour semantics) and screen-craft.md (the craft â€” spacing ladder, scaffolds, hierarchy, UX patterns, states, review pass). Installed into the consumer repo root so humans and AI sessions there inherit the rules. Release 2026-07-21.

files.content:
# Designing with Pucar Â· ON Court

> The **design-language skill** for the Pucar design system. Read this before designing or generating any screen.
> This is where design *policy* lives â€” edit it freely as taste evolves. The token/component **reference** is generated separately (see `llms.txt`); this file is the human-authored intent that `llms.txt`, `CLAUDE.md`, Figma and Pencil all point to.

---

## 0 Â· North star â€” the feel

Calm, dignified **public service**. A courts product used by citizens under stress and by staff all day. It must feel **friendly, not intimidating**, trustworthy, and quiet â€” *premium through restraint, never flashy*. When unsure, choose the calmer, more legible, more forgiving option. "Airy and a little apologetic."

---

## 1 Â· Non-negotiable rules

### Typography
- **Sentence case only.** Never ALL-CAPS or Title Case â€” including eyebrow/section labels, buttons, table headers, nav. **Exceptions:** proper nouns (ON Court, Pucar) and established legal abbreviations (CNR, FIR, IPC, S.138). *Why: caps shout, which intimidates; and Indic scripts have no case, so caps don't translate.*
- Body defaults to **16px** (reading + UI). Use 14 (`body-compact`) only for dense, staff-only data.
- **No thin text.** Honour the weight ladder (captionâ†’500, bodyâ†’400/500/600, titlesâ†’600). Sub-400 never ships.
- Hierarchy comes from **weight + `neutral-11`**, not from colour.

### Colour
- **Brand teal is rationed** â€” primary action, active state, focus. Never decoration.
- **Status colours mean status** (success / warning / info / destructive), never styling.
- **Each status hue has exactly three treatments** (ruling 2026-07-21): the **solid** (`success` + `success-foreground`) â€” a fill for the one strong action per view; the **tint pair** (`success-muted` + `success-muted-foreground`) â€” chips, callouts, flagged rows; the **ink** (`success-ink`) â€” status-coloured text and icons on neutral surfaces (page, card, wells). The solid is never a text colour; the ink is never a fill. Inks are engineered to clear 4.5:1 on the page *and* inside `surface-sunken` (amber-11 and blue-11 both missed, so `warning-ink`/`info-ink` are tuned â€” the amber stays amber rather than going brown-12).
- **No alpha status/brand fills.** A translucent tint's contrast depends on whatever sits underneath (`bg-destructive/10` measured 4.44:1 on `accent` â€” under the floor). Every tint that carries text is an opaque, gate-verified pair. Alpha remains legal in exactly three places: focus rings, shadows, and washes over *content that must stay readable through them* (document-annotation highlights, scrims).
- **Data-viz uses the categorical palette** (`chart-1â€¦5`) â€” chosen for mutual distinction, *not* status meaning.
- Neutrals carry structure (surfaces, borders, text).
- **Greys form one monotone fill ladder, each step one job** (re-ruled 2026-07-21 â€” the old "interactive stays lighter than structural" wording was internally false): `muted`/`sidebar` (2 â€” quiet washes) â†’ `surface-sunken` (tuned 2Â˝ â€” nested wells, separating on a card **with no border**) â†’ `accent` (3 â€” hover of white/transparent-rest controls, and the rest fill of `secondary`) â†’ `accent-strong` (4 â€” one step further: hover of grey-rest controls, pressed toggles, expanded triggers) â†’ `track` (5 â€” recessed control tracks, the darkest fill, so a white/teal active pops) â†’ borders (5/7) â†’ `input` (9). Interaction always moves **one step down the ladder** â€” a control never hovers to its own rest colour (the bug that made secondary-button hovers invisible). Never pick a raw `neutral-N` for any of these jobs.
- **Depth is fill, not repeated borders.** The outer card keeps the one hairline; nested wells use `surface-sunken` with **no border of their own** â€” a border on a nested item reads as box-in-box. Exception: small chips/thumbnails are defined *by* a border. **Never pick a raw `neutral-N` for any surface/track/accent** â€” change the one token and it propagates everywhere.
- **Never rely on colour alone.** Pair it with an icon, label, or arrow (e.g. â†‘/â†“ on deltas). *Colour-blind and bad-monitor safe.*

### Accessibility â€” the floor, not the aspiration
- **WCAG 2.2 AA:** text â‰Ą 4.5:1, large text / UI boundaries â‰Ą 3:1. **Verify, don't eyeball.**
- Interactive states must stay **perceptible** â€” hover/selection use `accent` (a calibrated light neutral, deliberately calm) or a brand tint, never pure page-white. The AA gate checks `accent-foreground` on `accent` in both themes.
- Focus is always visible (the teal ring). Don't remove it.
- Touch targets â‰Ą **40px** on citizen-facing surfaces.

### Spacing & density
- **Generous by default.** Whitespace is a feature, not waste â€” it lowers cognitive load and reads as calm and premium.
- 4px grid, 8px rhythm; the 2px nudge is optical-only, never layout.
- Earn density only where trained staff live in tables â€” via line-height and padding, never by shrinking type or targets.

### Elevation & shape
- Depth is **semantic**: flat (page), raised (cards, optional), overlay (popovers), modal (dialogs). No decorative shadows.
- One system radius (10px) and its derived scale. No loose values.
- **Radius is assigned by role, not by eye** (ruling 2026-07-21): container (card, dialog, bordered region) â†’ `xl` Â· control (button, field, track) â†’ `lg` Â· inset item (menu row, list row, inner button) â†’ `md`/`sm` Â· micro-control (checkbox) â†’ derived `sm` Â· **chip â†’ `full`, always**. Bare `rounded` (a fixed 4px that ignores the knob) is illegal. Siblings at the same anatomy level share one radius.

### Control metrics
- **Every field-like control defaults to 40px tall** â€” input, select, composed field (input-group), OTP slot, command input, tabs track. The size ladder is 32 / 36 / **40** / 44; smaller sizes are *opt-in* for staff-dense surfaces, never a primitive's default. Citizen-facing primary flows step up to 44 (`lg`).
- **Container padding is one number: 24px** (`p-6`) â€” card, dialog, sheet, drawer, nested well. The `sm` card variant (staff-dense) uses 16. Nothing else.
- **There is exactly one chip: `Badge`.** 24px tall, pill-shaped, caption type. Status variants (`success/warning/info/destructive`) use the opaque muted token pairs â€” never alpha fills, whose contrast shifts with the backdrop (`bg-destructive/10` measured 4.44:1 on `accent` â€” below AA).

---

## 2 Â· Information design
- **One primary line per row**; secondary info muted beneath it. *(A hearing row = parties in medium weight; CNR muted-mono below.)*
- Prefer **dividers over density**; keep table headers quiet (`muted-foreground`) so the *data* reads first.
- Numbers and IDs are tabular; CNRs are monospace.
- **Progressive disclosure** â€” show the essential, link to the rest ("4 of 27 shown â†’ View cause list").
- One obvious primary action per view. Reduce the number of choices on screen.

## 3 Â· Responsiveness
- **Mobile-first.** Sidebar collapses to a drawer; multi-column grids stack; touch targets stay â‰Ą 40px.
- Comfortable reading max-widths; tables scroll horizontally rather than crush.
- Test at **375 / 768 / 1280**.

## 4 Â· Robustness â€” the Pucar stress tests
- **Bad monitor:** if a state depends on a near-white grey, it fails. Calibrated greys / brand tints for anything interactive.
- **Low network:** system-font stack (zero download); avoid heavy assets.
- **Indic scripts:** sentence case; ensure Malayalam renders (OS fallback / bundled Noto Sans Malayalam).

## 5 Â· Choosing components
- Primary action â†’ `Button` default (teal). Secondary â†’ `outline` / `secondary`. Tertiary â†’ `ghost`.
- **Solid coloured action buttons** (`success` / `warning` / `info` / `destructive-solid`) are **rationed** â€” at most one strong coloured action per view, beside the single teal primary. Only status actions hover in-hue; neutral buttons (`outline` / `secondary` / `ghost`) hover grey *by design* â€” that's correct, not a bug.
- Status â†’ muted **pill** chips (rounded-full), AA text. Plain labels/metadata â†’ neutral chips.
- Cards default **flat** (hairline); use raised only to lift something genuinely above the page.
- Charts â†’ categorical palette; highlight the current / most-relevant series.
- Forms â†’ label above field, hint & error in `caption`; error uses `destructive` **plus text** (never colour alone).

## 6 Â· Do / don't
| âś… Do | âťŚ Don't |
|---|---|
| `New case` | `NEW CASE` Â· `New Case` |
| a ghost hover you can see on a cheap screen | a hover that vanishes into the background |
| `â†“ 3.1%` in red **with the arrow** | red text with no arrow |
| one teal primary action per view | teal sprinkled as decoration |
| roomy rows, quiet headers | a dense grid that reads like a spreadsheet |

---

*Source of design intent. `llms.txt` and `CLAUDE.md` reference this file, and it ships as a registry item â€” so any project or AI building with Pucar inherits these rules.*

# Screen craft â€” how to compose a screen that passes a professional eye > `design-guidelines.md` is the **law** (what and why). This file is the **craft** (how). > Read BOTH before building any screen. The law without the craft produces on-brand screens with amateur bones â€” wrong spacing, mushy hierarchy, missing states. This file exists because that happened. The rule underneath everything here: **never freestyle layout.** Start from a scaffold (Â§2), take every gap from the ladder (Â§1), run the review pass (Â§8) before calling anything done. --- ## 0 Â· Before you place anything Answer these in one line each â€” in a comment or to yourself. If you can't, stop and ask: 1. **The job:** what does the user come to this screen to do? One sentence. 2. **The one action:** which single button is THE primary action? (Everything else is outline/ghost/link.) 3. **The mode:** citizen (stressed, occasional, mobile-likely â†’ generous, one decision at a time) or staff (trained, all-day, data-dense â†’ earned density)? This sets widths, spacing, and target sizes for the whole screen (Â§7). 4. **The hierarchy:** what must be seen 1st, 2nd, 3rd? If everything feels important, nothing is â€” cut or demote. ## 1 Â· Spacing â€” proximity is meaning Space is not decoration; it *is* the grouping structure. Related things sit closer than unrelated things â€” that single principle produces most of "professional". **The ladder â€” these gaps only.** Tailwind: `1=4px 2=8px 3=12px 4=16px 6=24px 8=32px 12=48px 16=64px`. Never arbitrary values (`p-[13px]`, `mt-5`, `gap-7` are all bugs). If a design feels like it needs an in-between value, the grouping is wrong, not the ladder. **Micro-spacing addendum (ruling 2026-07-21):** *inside* a single control or chip â€” iconâ†”label, chip padding, track inset â€” the half-steps `0.5=2px 1.5=6px 2.5=10px` are legal, because text metrics need them. They are **never** legal *between* elements: layout gaps come only from the ladder above. (`gap-1.5` inside a button: fine. `space-y-1.5` between a title and subtitle: fine, that pair is one lockup. `gap-2.5` between two cards: bug.) **Distance encodes relationship:** | Relationship | Gap | |---|---| | Inside an element (icon â†” its text) | `gap-1.5` / `gap-2` | | Label â†” its field Â· title â†” its subtitle | `gap-1.5` / `gap-2` | | Field â†” next field in the same group | `gap-4` â€¦ `gap-6` | | Card â†” card Â· group â†” group | `gap-6` | | Section â†” section on a page | `space-y-8` (staff) / `space-y-10`â€“`12` (citizen) | | Page header â†” first content | `mb-8` | **Container padding:** cards `p-6` (never less than the largest gap inside them); page gutters `px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8`, vertical `py-8`; dialog/sheet content `p-6`. **Three checks that catch 90% of spacing bugs:** - **Padding â‰Ą gap** â€” a container's padding is never smaller than the gaps inside it (otherwise content hugs the edge while floating apart internally). - **Outside > inside** â€” the space *around* a group is always larger than the spaces *within* it. If `gap` between groups â‰¤ `gap` within groups, grouping is illegible. - **Symmetry** â€” left/right padding equal; the same section rhythm all the way down the page. One inconsistent margin reads as a mistake everywhere. When something feels cramped, add space **between groups**, not inside elements. ## 2 Â· Scaffolds â€” start from a skeleton, never a blank file Pick the matching skeleton and fill it. These encode the decisions that keep screens consistent. **Every page:** ```tsx
{/* header block */}

Hearings today

{/* citizen pages: text-title */}

Supporting line â€” what this screen is, in one sentence.

{/* THE primary action â€” exactly one, teal */}
{/* sections */}
``` Widths: staff/table pages `max-w-6xl`â€“`7xl` Â· citizen flows and forms `max-w-2xl`â€“`3xl` Â· reading content `max-w-prose`. Never stretch a form to fill a wide screen. **Form screen** (`max-w-2xl`): sections as cards or `space-y-10` groups. Each section = `title-s` heading + optional `caption` muted description + fields in `space-y-6`. **Single column always** â€” two-column forms cause skipped fields and broken tab order; the only exception is tightly-coupled pairs (city/PIN) as `grid grid-cols-2 gap-4`. Label above field (`gap-2`); hint/error in `caption` under the field. Actions in a row at the end: `
` â€” ghost/outline secondary first, primary last (outer position). Long legal forms: end with a **review step**, not a surprise submit. **List/table screen:** toolbar (`flex items-center justify-between gap-4 mb-6`: search left â€” labeled, `sr-only` is fine; filters middle; primary action right) â†’ table in a flat card â†’ footer with count + pagination. Row anatomy: one primary line (`font-medium`) + one muted secondary line (CNR in `font-mono text-muted-foreground`); headers quiet (`text-muted-foreground`, sentence case); row actions as a ghost `â€¦` menu at row end; whole row clickable to detail. **Detail screen:** identity header (title + status pill inline, muted mono meta line below, actions right) â†’ `grid gap-6 lg:grid-cols-3`: main content `lg:col-span-2`, aside (parties, dates, documents) in the third. Sections inside use the same `space-y-8`. **Dashboard:** stat row `grid gap-6 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4`; each stat card = `caption` muted label â†’ number in `text-title tabular-nums` â†’ delta with **arrow + colour** (never colour alone). Then chart + list pairings, each in a card with a quiet `title-s` header row. **Empty state (its own component shape, centered â€” the one place centering is right):** ```tsx

No cases yet

When you file a complaint, it appears here with its hearing dates.

``` ## 3 Â· Hierarchy â€” the squint test - **One `

` per screen.** The ladder: page title `title-l` (staff) / `title` (citizen) â†’ section `title-s` â†’ card/group title `text-body font-medium` â†’ content `body` / `body-compact` â†’ metadata `caption text-muted-foreground`. Don't invent intermediate sizes; don't skip levels. - **At most 3 levels of emphasis visible at once.** Emphasis comes from **weight (500/600) and muting (`text-muted-foreground`)** â€” never from size sprawl or colour (law: hierarchy = weight + neutral-11). - **Elevate the one by muting the many.** Labels, IDs, timestamps, helper text â†’ muted. The *data* is the figure; everything else is ground. If a row/card has two bold things, one of them is wrong. - Numbers align in columns: `tabular-nums`. Identifiers (CNR, case no.): `font-mono text-muted-foreground`. - **The squint test:** blur your eyes at the rendered screen. You should still find: the page title, the primary action, and the first row/card of real content. If anything else competes, demote it. ## 4 Â· Alignment & shape - **One left spine.** Page title, section headings, card contents, and form labels all start at the same x-position. Mixed indents read as sloppiness even when nobody can name why. - **Center only** empty states and single-purpose confirmation moments. Never center body text or form labels. - Icon + text: `flex items-center gap-2`, icon `size-4` matched to line-height. Buttons in a row share ONE size. - Inputs in a group share one width; never a ragged right edge of mixed-width fields. - Radius and elevation are semantic (law Â§Elevation): cards flat-with-hairline by default, `shadow-raised` only for genuine lift, overlays/modals use their own levels. No decorative shadows, no mixed radii. ## 5 Â· UX patterns â€” when to reach for what **Dialog vs sheet vs page:** dialog = one decision or a tiny form (â‰¤ ~4 fields); sheet = contextual create/edit while the list stays visible behind; full page = anything multi-section, anything with legal weight (filings are pages, not popups). Destructive confirms = `alert-dialog`, always naming the object and the consequence ("Delete draft complaint against Suresh Kumar? This can't be undone.") with `destructive-solid` confirm. **Feedback:** background/async results â†’ toast (sonner). Blocking context the user must read â†’ inline `Alert` above the content it blocks. Field problems â†’ inline error under the field (`caption` + destructive + text, never colour alone). Never a toast for a field error; never an alert for a success. **Filtering & search:** active filters render as removable chips under the toolbar; always show a result count ("14 cases"); filtered-empty gets its own empty state with "Clear filters", distinct from true-empty. **Progressive disclosure:** show the essential, link the rest ("4 of 27 shown â†’ View cause list"). Rare/advanced form fields behind a collapsible, not deleted. Critical info never lives *only* in a tooltip or hover. **Status & time:** state â†’ muted pill; case progress â†’ vertical timeline (past = muted dot, current = brand, future = hollow); neutral metadata â†’ plain muted text, not a pill. Pill inflation is real â€” if everything has a pill, nothing has status. **Selection density:** â‰¤5 options â†’ radio group / toggle group (visible, one tap); >5 â†’ select; multi-select from many â†’ checkboxes in a scroll area, chosen items echoed as chips. **Loading:** skeletons that **mirror the final layout** (same heights, same grid â€” no layout jump), never a bare centered spinner for a whole page. Buttons show in-button pending state on submit. ## 6 Â· A screen is five screens â€” states are the design Nothing is "done" with only the happy path. Every screen ships with: 1. **Default** â€” real-shaped data, not lorem ipsum (use plausible Kerala names, real CNR formats). 2. **Empty** â€” first-run teaches the first action (Â§2 recipe). Filtered-empty is a separate, lighter state. 3. **Loading** â€” skeleton mirroring layout. 4. **Error** â€” what happened + what to do now, in plain sentences ("Couldn't load today's hearings. Check your connection and retry."). Never a bare code. 5. **Edge** â€” the longest party name you can imagine (and its Malayalam version), a 16-char CNR, 0 items, 1 item, 10,000 items, tomorrow's date, a hearing 4 years out. Truncate deliberately (`truncate` + full value on hover/detail), wrap intentionally, and make sure the layout survives all of it. ## 7 Â· Density modes (from Â§0's answer) - **Citizen:** `max-w-2xl/3xl` Â· targets â‰Ą 44px (`size="lg"` for primary flows) Â· `body` 16 everywhere Â· `space-y-10`+ between sections Â· one decision per screen where possible Â· progress indicator on multi-step flows. - **Staff:** density earned in **tables only**, via row padding and line-height â€” never by shrinking type below `body-compact` (14) or targets below 40px. Page furniture (headers, toolbars) keeps the generous rhythm even on dense screens. ## 8 Â· The review pass â€” run this before declaring done Go through literally, against the rendered screen (light AND dark, 375/768/1280): - [ ] Every gap/padding is from the ladder â€” zero arbitrary values in the diff - [ ] Padding â‰Ą gap in every container; outside > inside for every group - [ ] Exactly one primary (teal) action; everything else outline/ghost/link - [ ] Exactly one `

`; type ladder with no skips; â‰¤3 emphasis levels per view - [ ] Squint test: title, primary action, first content â€” findable blurred - [ ] One left spine; forms single-column at `max-w-2xl`; no full-width inputs on wide screens - [ ] All five states exist (default/empty/loading/error/edge) and the layout survives the edge kit - [ ] Status has icon-or-text with its colour; deltas have arrows; sentence case everywhere - [ ] Focus visible on every interactive element by keyboard-tabbing through - [ ] Dark mode actually checked, not assumed If any box fails, fix it before showing the screen. This pass is not optional polish â€” it's the difference this file exists to make. --- *Companion to `design-guidelines.md` (the law) and `CLAUDE.md` (the workflow). Ships with the repo so every human and AI designing with Pucar composes screens the same way.*
